rulings.Case Title: LISA WALKER VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Order To Compel Depositions And Documents From Defts, And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, November 27, 2013, Line 14, CROSS COMPLAINANT JAMES MEISENBACH's Motion For Protective Order To Compel Depositions And Documents From Defendants, And Request For Monetary Sanctions. The motion is granted as follows: (1) Counsel for parties will meet and confer on a schedule for the depositions of Plaintiff Walker and City Employees Sue Grenci, Tom Carte, Lavena Holmes, Magaly Fernandez and Micki Callahan. (2) No less than five days priort to the first deposition, Defendant City and County of San Francisco will make full and complete production of all documents responsive to Defendant and Cross-Complainant James Mesienbach's Request for Production of Documetns Set One. Such production shall be made without objection except as to attorney/client privilege and, to the extent that any documents are witheld on the bassis they will be identified with specific detail to allow a challege to be made to the claim of privilege. (3) The Court will impose monetary sacntions in the amount of $500.00 against the City and County of San Francisco, payable within 10 days form the date of this Order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: ANNA POLISHCHUK VS. CAMDEN MINERVINO et al*****CASE TRANSFERRED TO SANTA CLARA COUNTY*****
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Change Venue
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, November 27, 2013, Line 7, DEFENDANT CAMDEN MINERVINO's Motion To Change Venue. Defendant's motion to transfer venue to Santa Clara County is granted. Defendant's request for judicial notice is granted. The motion is timely, as it was filed within a reasonable time after the answer was filed. (See Cooney v. Cooney (1944) 25 Cal.2d 202, 208.) Defendant produces sufficient evidence from non-party witnesses regarding the reasons why San Francisco County would be inconvenient and why the ends of justice would be promoted if the Court granted the transfer motion. Furthermore, the record shows that the Defendant corporation has been dismissed from the case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: PAVLIN H ZANEV et al VS. SANDRA D FULLERTON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff To Comply With Demand For Inspection To Permit Defendant'S Experts To Take Vehicle For Test Ride
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, November 27, 2013, Line 3 - DEFENDANT SANDRA FULLERTON's Motion To Compel Plaintiff To Comply With Demand For Inspection To Permit Defendant'S Experts To Take Vehicle For Test Ride. Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Court finds that good cause has been established for the road test, therefore, motion granted, with the caveat that the road test must be completed on a private driveway or in a private parking lot immediately adjacent to the facility where the scooter in question is stored, or at another location mutually agreed upon by the parties. Unless otherwise agreed, the road test shall be completed no later than December 6, 2013 due to the rapidly approaching trial date. The Court notes that while Plaintiff raises a legitimate concern about potential injuries or damage occurring during the test, Defendant has provided liability and indemnity waivers. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to davidmcdonald@prodigy.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: KELLY O'HAIRE VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant City And County Of San Francisco'S Demurrer To 1st Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, November 27, 2013, Line 15, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S Demurrer To 1st Amended Complaint. CCSF's demurrer to the retaliation cause of action is overruled. Plaintiff sufficiently alleges protected activity and plaintiff did not need to exhaust her administrative remedies. See Lloyd v. County of LA (2009) 172 Cal.App.4th 320. The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend on the retaliation cause of action as to Chief Suhr because he was not plaintiff's employer. The Court overrules the demurrer to the intentional infliction of emotional distress cause of action. The demurrer to the cause of action for intentional interference with prospective advantage is sustained with leave to amend to plead facts regarding an economic relationship between the plaintiff and some third party, with the probability of future economic benefit to the plaintiff. The motion to strike is denied. The allegations do not clearly implicate confidential information from Chief Suhr's personnel file. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: LINDA A HESS VS. EXPERIAN CREDIT REPORTING INFO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, November 27, 2013, Line 12, DEFENDANT GMAC MORTGAGE LLC's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. The demurrer to the 1st cause of action is sustained with 20 days leave to amend. Plaintiff has leave to plead what section of the Consumer Reporting Agencies Act she seeks to recover under. In addition, if plaintiff seeks relief under the Investigative Consumer Reporting Agencies Act-a separate statutory scheme-plaintiff should allege that claim as a distinct cause of action. The demurrer to the declaratory relief cause of action is overruled. The claim is not necessarily preempted and Plaintiff alleges an actual controversy. The cause of action is not duplicative. The Court sustains with 20 days leave to amend the demurrer to the accounting cause of action. To state a cause of action, only the simplest pleading is required: (1) The fiduciary relationship or other circumstances appropriate to the remedy [citation], [and] (2) A balance due from the defendant to the plaintiff that can only be ascertained by an accounting." (5 Witkin, Cal. Proc. (5th ed. 2008) Pleading, § 820, p. 236.) Plaintiff does not allege that a balance is due from Defendant to Plaintiff. (Complaint, 17.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: LINDA A HESS VS. EXPERIAN CREDIT REPORTING INFO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, November 27, 2013, Line 12, DEFENDANT SAXON MORTGAGE SERVICES, INC.'s DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. The demurrer to the first cause of action is sustained with 20 days leave to amend. Plaintiff has leave to allege facts regarding how defendant violated the Consumer Reporting Agencies Act or the Investigative Consumer Reporting Agencies Act. If plaintiff alleges that defendant breached both statutory schemes, plaintiff should allege two distinct causes of action. The demurrer to the declaratory relief cause of action is sustained without leave to amend. Judicially noticeable documents show that plaintiff did in fact have an account with defendant. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: YELIZAVETA LIVSHITS VS. TAYLOR K. SMITH MD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, November 27, 2013, Line 13, DEFENDANTs CALIFORNIA PACIFIC ORTHOPAEDIC AND SPORTS MEDICINE and TAYLOR SMITH, M.D.'s MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. The Motion to Strike is granted in part and overruled in part. The Motion is granted with respect to the prayer for punitive damages, because CCP § 425.13 applies to claims for battery that arise from the rendition of medical services. (See, Central Pathology Service Medical Clinic, Inc. v. Superior Court (1992) 3 Cal.4th 181, 192.) The Motion to Strike the "Medical Battery" language is denied, as Plaintiff has properly stated a claim for Medical Battery. (See, Saxena v. Goffney (2008) 159 Cal.App.4th 316, 324; Complaint 28-30.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: YELIZAVETA LIVSHITS VS. TAYLOR K. SMITH MD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, November 27, 2013, Line 13, DEFENDANTs CALIFORNIA PACIFIC ORTHOPAEDIC AND SPORTS MEDICINE and TAYLOR SMITH, M.D.'s DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. The demurrer is overruled in its entirety. The cause of action for Elder Abuse is not time-barred. (See Cal. Code Civ. Proc., § 335.1; Norgart v. Upjohn Co. (1999) 21 Cal.4th 383.) Plaintiff has properly pled that Defendants failed to provide medical care while Plaintiff was in Defendants' care and custody. (See, Complaint, 9-10.) Plaintiff need not plead inability to care for her own basic needs to establish a claim for Elder Abuse. (See, Welf. & Inst. Code, § 15600 et seq.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
